ops.text: cc-notes sync (delta AND --full) reports all-zeros against a remote whose ref exists but whose LFS attachment blob was never uploaded (log 5e69ae9's "ccx.py", oid a49b73d3…): the publisher pushed refs/cc-notes/* via plain git push, CI's reconcile then 404'd on download, yet a local sync from a machine holding the blob in .git/lfs/objects considers there is nothing to upload — presumably ref-delta-driven, assuming ref-present ⇒ content-present. Repair required hand-running `git lfs push --object-id origin <oid>`. sync --full should verify remote existence of every attachment oid referenced by pushed refs (or at least offer a --verify-attachments mode), since the error message's own suggested fix ("ask the publisher to run cc-notes sync") does nothing in exactly this state.
